bullets.text: Israeli strikes in Lebanon killed nine people, while Hezbollah launched rockets over the border, escalating tensions and straining a recent ceasefire.
Iranian drone strikes on Kuwait airport killed one person and injured dozens, with Iran stating the attack was in retaliation for earlier US strikes.
A new Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo may have started as early as January, according to the WHO chief, who noted the virus had a significant head start.
Popular Kakadu attractions Jim Jim Falls and Twin Falls will remain closed for the rest of the year, leading to concerns among tourism operators about the economic impact.
A repeat child sex offender was mistakenly awarded Victoria's "disability pride champion" title due to an administrative error by a government department.
A daughter is pleading to prevent her father's deportation to Nauru, a place she describes as a site of "final and lifelong punishment," after he was released from indefinite immigration detention.
US oil stocks have dropped to their lowest level since 2004, a situation industry experts warn could lead to a jump in prices as inventories reach a critically low threshold.
Bill Ackman's Pershing Square is reportedly planning to sell its stake in Universal Music Group NV, just days after the company rejected a takeover bid from the hedge fund billionaire.
SpaceX is seeking to raise $75 billion in an initial public offering, which would be the largest ever, to fund its initiatives in AI and space launches.
